trial_title: A Phase III, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Multicenter Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of an Investigational Anti-PCSK9 Monoclonal Antibody in Statin-Intolerant Patients with Heterozygous Familial Hypercholesterolaemia
intervention: Anti-PCSK9 Monoclonal Antibody (fully human IgG1, 150 mg SC every 4 weeks)
indication: Heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia (HeFH) in statin-intolerant patients
summary: This is a Phase III clinical study protocol (Version 2.0, Amendment 1) evaluating an investigational anti-PCSK9 monoclonal antibody administered subcutaneously every 4 weeks in statin-intolerant patients with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia. The randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled multicenter trial aims to enroll 360 patients and assess percent change in LDL-C at Week 24 as the primary endpoint, with 52 weeks of treatment and 24 weeks of follow-up. The protocol includes comprehensive study design, patient eligibility criteria, assessments, statistical analysis plans, and ethical considerations.
references_to: Simon Broome or Dutch Lipid Clinic criteria, score >=6
Phase I data in healthy volunteers
